items.headline: This Would Be Genocide
items.summary: Trump's declaration that “a whole civilization will die tonight" went a step farther than even he has ever gone before.

items.headline: Gulf leaders tell Trump: No ceasefire without ending Iranian threat
items.summary: With about a day left before the deadline expires, the US president held a marathon of calls with regional leaders, including Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. At the center of the talks was the possibility of a significant escalation in the fighting against…

items.headline: Negative views of Israel, Netanyahu continue to rise among Americans – especially young people
items.summary: Views of Israel and Benjamin Netanyahu have grown more negative among U.S. adults, with majorities under 50 in both parties rating them poorly.

items.headline: China and Russia raise their hands at the UN conference, stating that saving only Hormuz is pointless.

items.headline: Viral research firm Citrini sent an analyst to the Strait of Hormuz. From smugglers to Iranian drones, here's what they found.
items.summary: Citrini Research's "Analyst #3" went to the Strait of Hormuz with recording equipment, "a pack of Cuban cigars, $15,000 in cash and a roll of Zyn."

items.headline: Iran Is Not Blocking the Strait of Hormuz. It's Running a Toll Booth

items.headline: Trump's shipping waiver does not boost oil flows within US; fuel exports soar
items.summary: Last month, Trump waived Jones Act limitations for 60 days starting March 17, hoping the move would help tame the ‌surge in fuel prices caused by the Iran war by increasing shipments from the US Gulf Coast to other coastal markets in the country

items.headline: What is Benjamin Netanyahu’s end game in the Iran war?
items.summary: The war in the Middle East is a huge political book for the Israeli prime minister in an election year – but it may come at a great cost.

items.headline: Sign Of The Times
items.summary: If you haven’t seen the movie, Project Hail Mary, then you should… it is excellent. It is faithful to Andy Weir’s 2021 book, hilarious and heart-warming, and with the Artemis II crew circling the moon, the film is just what we needed.

items.headline: 'Painful' interest rates are looming with the world facing a new wave of inflationary pressure, Harvard economist says
items.summary: Kenneth Rogoff said the latest oil-price spike is adding to a host of inflationary pressures facing the global economy.

items.headline: Australia Fuel Prices Ease Slightly to ~$2.40/L After Excise Cuts, But Diesel Soars Amid Iran War Fallout
items.summary: SYDNEY — Australian fuel prices have begun to moderate in early April 2026 after the federal government's temporary halving of the fuel excise and an additional cut funded by GST revenue, yet they remain significantly higher than pre-conflict levels due to on…

items.headline: Diesel Prices Exceed $8 Per Gallon in San Francisco as Supply Disruptions Affect California

items.headline: Oil Price Shocks Make Brazil Key to Energy Security in the Americas
items.summary: Oil price shocks since the U.S. and Israel first struck Iran, the world’s fifth-largest oil producer, have global markets on edge. The sudden energy crisis, which emerged after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz, preventing the passage of around a fifth of the …

items.summary:

items.headline: Russia Expands Gasoline Export Ban, Faces Major Oil Outages Due to Drone Strikes
items.summary: The Russian government has expanded a ban on gasoline exports to include producers of the fuel, according to a report from Interfax news agency [1]. The ban, which now includes all gasoline producers, is scheduled to remain in effect until July 31. It expands…

items.headline: Here’s a List of Gulf Energy Infrastructure Damaged in Iran War
items.summary: Dozens of refineries, oil fields, gas plants, ports and other energy infrastructure have been damaged by missile and drone strikes, five weeks into the Iran war. US President Donald Trump threatened to bring “Hell” to Iran with strikes on power …

items.headline: Ukraine Steps Up Drone Attacks on Russian Oil Infrastructure
items.summary: The key Russian oil export terminal at Novorossiysk was on fire early on Monday following an overnight drone attack, as Ukraine intensifies strikes on Russia’s oil infrastructure to reduce its export capabilities amid surging oil prices. The area at the port …

items.headline: Three killed in Russian strike on Odesa as Ukraine hits Russian oil sites
items.summary: Russian drone strikes in Odesa claimed civilian lives including a toddler. Ukrainian drones retaliated targeting Russia's oil export ports.  The attacks damaged infrastructure and injured civilians. Ukraine faces ongoing challenges with air defense.  Russia c…

items.headline: Trump's 'English Obscenities' to Iran - Latest War News
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has warned Iran that if an agreement is not reached within the next 48 hours, he will launch severe attacks on Iran's energy and transportation infrastructure, forcing Iran to allow ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. Trump, u…

items.headline: IAEA Director General Warns of Radiological Release Risk Amid Iran War
items.summary: IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi told the Board of Governors that the ongoing military attacks on Iran could result in radiological releases requiring evacuations of areas as large as major cities. The Agency has no indication that nuclear installations have been damaged but has lost communication with Iranian authorities.

items.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei Confirms Iran's New Supreme Leader Alive After Ceasefire Deal
items.summary: Iranian state media confirmed Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is alive and made the decision to offer a ceasefire deal Monday night, despite questions about his health since the war began when family members were killed.

dimension_signal.rationale: Survival pressures eased by successful ceasefire negotiation despite family losses

items.headline: Field Marshal Asim Munir's Nuclear Threat Signals Pakistan Escalation Doctrine Shift
items.summary: Pakistan's Field Marshal Asim Munir allegedly stated 'We are a nuclear nation; if we think we are going down, we'll take half the world down with us' and threatened to 'start from India's East.' India called it nuclear sabre-rattling.

dimension_signal.rationale: Unprecedented nuclear threats indicate heightened impulsive decision-making under pressure

items.headline: Xi Jinping Accelerates Nuclear Expansion in Response to Iran War
items.summary: Experts assess that the US-Israel war on Iran has hardened China's resolve to expand nuclear weapons programs, with Xi Jinping's 2027 military goals now including nuclear weapons as core to constraining American options over Taiwan.

dimension_signal.rationale: Nuclear expansion shows adaptive survival strategy in response to changing threat environment

items.headline: ICC Maintains Netanyahu Arrest Warrant Despite Israeli Challenge
items.summary: The International Criminal Court rejected Israeli bid to halt Gaza probe and maintains arrest warrants for Prime Minister Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Gallant, finding reasonable grounds they committed war crimes.

dimension_signal.rationale: International legal pressure maintained despite Israeli diplomatic efforts

items.headline: Pakistan Deploys 'Ghatak' Border Action Teams Against India
items.summary: Pakistan's military under Field Marshal Munir has allegedly deployed specialized 'ghatak' (killer) units along Jammu-Rajouri border, planning Border Action Team attacks targeting Indian Army personnel despite economic crisis.

items.headline: CENTCOM Chief Briefs Trump on Iran Strike Options After Nuclear Talks Stall
items.summary: CENTCOM Admiral Brad Cooper briefed President Trump on military options ranging from limited strikes on nuclear sites to broader operations carrying risks of escalation and regime change, after Iran rejected major US proposals.

dimension_signal.rationale: Considering broader military operations despite diplomatic channels still open

items.headline: Taiwan Intelligence: Xi Jinping Purges 983,000 Officials Ahead of 2027 Taiwan Deadline
items.summary: Taiwan's National Security Bureau assessed China's Communist Party disciplined nearly one million officials in 2025, with 87% of PLA generals since 2022 purged as Xi prepares military for potential Taiwan invasion by 2027.

dimension_signal.rationale: Massive purges indicate Xi consolidating control but also reveal internal resistance

items.headline: China-Pakistan Joint Peace Plan for Iran War Aims to Project Regional Influence
items.summary: China and Pakistan announced joint five-point peace plan for Iran War following meeting between Foreign Ministers Wang Yi and Ishaq Dar, as Beijing seeks to portray itself as mediator while protecting economic interests.

items.headline: Pakistan's 'Islamic NATO' Strategy Fails as Regional Isolation Deepens
items.summary: Field Marshal Asim Munir's ambition to position Pakistan as security leader in Muslim world faces harsh reality as ongoing wars and domestic pressures expose limits of 'Islamic NATO' strategy, leaving Pakistan increasingly isolated.

dimension_signal.rationale: Strategic isolation increases survival pressures despite field marshal promotion

items.headline: IAEA Reports 440kg of Iranian Enriched Uranium Status Unknown Since War Began
items.summary: IAEA Director General Grossi confirmed the Agency cannot verify Iran's stockpile of 440.9kg of 60% enriched uranium since US-Israeli strikes, with inspectors observing vehicular activity at bombed sites but no access granted.

items.headline: Israeli Strikes Hit Iranian Nuclear Sites Including Ardakan Yellowcake Facility
items.summary: IAEA confirmed attacks on Iran's Shahid Rezayee Nejad Yellow Cake Production Facility (Ardakan) and severe damage to Khondab heavy water plant, which is no longer operational but contained no declared nuclear material.
